ddls:
CREATE TABLE "libraries" (
  "library_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each library or tool */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the library or tool */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the library or tool */,
  "odata_versions" TEXT /* Supported OData version(s) */,
  "usage" TEXT /* Indicates whether the library is for server-side or client-side */,
  "download_location" TEXT /* Location from where the library or tool can be downloaded */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the library */,
  "published_date" TEXT /* Date when the library was published */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date of the last update to the library */,
  "license_type" TEXT /* Type of license (e.g., MIT, Apache) */,
  "platforms_supported" TEXT /* Platforms where the library can be used (e.g., Windows, Linux, macOS) */,
  "documentation_link" TEXT /* Link to the library's documentation */,
  "support_link" TEXT /* Link to the library's support resources */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags for easy search and categorization */,
  "star_count" INTEGER /* Number of stars or likes the library has received */,
  "fork_count" INTEGER /* Number of forks the library has received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("library_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "downloads" (
  "download_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each download event */,
  "library_id" INTEGER /* ID of the library or tool that was downloaded */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who downloaded the library or tool */,
  "download_date" TEXT /* Date when the library or tool was downloaded */,
  "download_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the library or tool has been downloaded */,
  "version_downloaded" TEXT /* Version of the library that was downloaded */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device from which the download occurred (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system used for the download */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location (geographic) of the download */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who downloaded the library */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser or client */,
  "download_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the download process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("download_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_downloads_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_downloads_library_id FOREIGN KEY ("library_id") REFERENCES libraries ("library_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* Link to the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief bio or description of the user */,
  "is_developer" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user is a developer */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who gave the rating */,
  "library_id" INTEGER /* ID of the library that was rated */,
  "rating_value" INTEGER /* The rating value (e.g., 1 to 5 stars) */,
  "review" TEXT /* A text review provided by the user */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_library_id FOREIGN KEY ("library_id") REFERENCES libraries ("library_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "usage_statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage statistic */,
  "library_id" INTEGER /* ID of the library */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date for which the statistic is recorded */,
  "total_downloads" INTEGER /* Total number of downloads on that date */,
  "unique_users" INTEGER /* Number of unique users who downloaded the library */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the library on that date */,
  "total_forks" INTEGER /* Total number of forks on that date */,
  "total_stars" INTEGER /* Total number of stars on that date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_usage_statistics_library_id FOREIGN KEY ("library_id") REFERENCES libraries ("library_id")
)
